newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The record backlog ($1.50B), bookings and earnings beat plus raised outlook were from the prior quarter and already known; the Jefferies PT raise to $105 (June 1) and the $60M/$12.3M contract wins predate the window. Stock hit an all-time high (~$122) on June 15 on momentum, not fresh news.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent under-covered AI supply chain: defense/space/edge compute thesis (rugged edge processing, RF, mission-compute with AMD Versal FPGAs) intact and still under-covered. No new datapoint since June 9; record backlog already priced. Operating turnaround keeps conviction speculative. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Defense AI needs deployable edge compute and sensor processing; record $1.5B backlog and improving margins support a turnaround.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically relevant but execution-show-me; backlog already reflected in price.
verdicts.bear: Prior execution issues keep the crowd skeptical; +49% YTD near all-time high embeds the recovery.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Defense electronics recovery and edge-AI module ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Operating-turnaround execution and mix shift off low-margin legacy
